title: From Control To Surrender
study: A Study In The Life Of King David
scriptureForWeek: 1 SAMUEL 16:1-13 Now the Lord said to Samuel, “You have mourned long enough for Saul. I have rejected him as king of Israel, so fill your flask with olive oil and go to Bethlehem. Find a man named Jesse who lives there, for I have selected one of his sons to be my king.” But Samuel asked, “How can I do that? If Saul hears about it, he will kill me.” “Take a heifer with you,” the Lord replied, “and say that you have come to make a sacrifice to the Lord. Invite Jesse to the sacrifice, and I will show you which of his sons to anoint for me.” So Samuel did as the Lord instructed. When he arrived at Bethlehem, the elders of the town came trembling to meet him. “What’s wrong?” they asked. “Do you come in peace?” “Yes,” Samuel replied. “I have come to sacrifice to the Lord. Purify yourselves and come with me to the sacrifice.” Then Samuel performed the purification rite for Jesse and his sons and invited them to the sacrifice, too. When they arrived, Samuel took one look at Eliab and thought, “Surely this is the Lord’s anointed!” But the Lord said to Samuel, “Don’t judge by his appearance or height, for I have rejected him. The Lorddoesn’t see things the way you see them. People judge by outward appearance, but the Lord looks at the heart.” Then Jesse told his son Abinadab to step forward and walk in front of Samuel. But Samuel said, “This is not the one the Lord has chosen.” Next Jesse summoned Shimea,but Samuel said, “Neither is this the one the Lord has chosen.” In the same way all seven of Jesse’s sons were presented to
weekTheme: Boys want man's attention, Men want God's attention God will see our hearts The world's value system for men these days is completely distorted. The men who get attention are not valued for their integrity, character, or faithfulness. The men who get attention today are valued for their talents, gifts, and accomplishments. No one really cares about the inner life of men today - their motives, their hearts, their faith. They seem to only care about the externals - how strong, jacked, or successful they are. But focusing on these outward things has really screwed up our world. A lot of famous people are failures in their personal lives. They have success by what they have done, not success in who they have become. God cares the most about our inner lives. He cares about our hearts as much as he cares about our gifts. He cares about who you become, not just what you do. If you chase attention by focusing on the outward elements of your life, it will sabotage the kind of life you live over the long haul. Way better to worry about what God thinks of you, than to have everyone around you love you. King David worried about his motives and his heart more than being popular. He sought to please God in private before being loved in public. And this ultimately showed that God could trust him with power, without that power destroying his life. This week, you are going to learn about what God looks for in a man, and how he can elevate us if he can trust us. This week’s text is an amazing story that should give you focus and hope as you face peer pressure to fit in or as you feel anxiety about being left out. Let's go.

days.title: Never Forgotten
days.scripture: 1 SAMUEL 16:10-11 In the same way all seven of Jesse’s sons were presented to Samuel. But Samuel said to Jesse, “The LORD has not chosen any of these.” Then Samuel asked, “Are these all the sons you have?” “There is still the youngest,” Jesse replied. “But he’s out in the fields watching the sheep and goats.” “Send for him at once,” Samuel said. “We will not sit down to eat until he arrives.”
days.reflection: Everyone wants attention. No one wants to be left out and overlooked. People can do crazy things to try and get attention. They can do dumb stunts and post them on social media. They can show off in front of their friends to make others think they are cool. The more insecure a person is, the more they need approval and validation from others. It's like there is a leaking balloon in our hearts that makes us have to get more and more attention to keep us inflated. This can be so exhausting and cause so much compromise and regret. In your grandparent’s generation, only 2 percent of people wanted to be famous. But today, more than a third of 18 to 24-year-olds say fame is important to them. Fame can have some real perks. Kim Kardashian gets paid 1.4 million dollars per Instagram post. (Bieber only gets 1.1 million per post… poor guy.) And it probably feels awesome being a household name. But it comes with a downside. Multiple divorces, longing for privacy, and obsessing over staying relevant can distort people’s lives and be so exhausting. King David ended up becoming the most famous man in his time, but it was never something he was chasing. He wasn’t seeking it, though it happened to him. He was more interested in growing in his relationship with God and surrendering the details to him. David was overlooked by his family. In fact, his dad didn’t even bother calling him in when the prophet Samuel showed up to anoint a new king as Saul’s replacement. Yet that didn’t seem to bother him. David was content living before God. David was content learning how to do his job as a shepherd well. David didn’t need the approval of those around him for his sense of worth. You live in a time of history where you will be tempted to try and get attention all the time. Social media can make you think that you have to have likes, followers, or tons of comments to fit in. But most of this is just an illusion, even if it feels rewarding at the moment. David teaches you how to care about something better than that - having the attention of heaven. Doing what God wants frees you from having to get validation from what people want. Jesus promises that he will never leave us or forsake us and that he will be with us always. You are never overlooked by God, even if other guys don’t notice you. You are never alone, even if you feel left out. You do matter, God sees you, and you will not be forgotten by him. What a relief; what good news. Learn to enjoy and celebrate that.
questions: Is there something that happened recently when you felt left out or overlooked? What was it? How did this make you feel?
Do you think social media like Instagram or Tik-Tok is good for guys your age? Why or why not?
Ask the person you are doing this study with how they learned to be content without being the center of attention. How have they overcome the need for attention?

thoughts.verse: The eyes of the LORD watch over those who do right; his ears are open to their cries for help.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to free you from the need for others’ attention. Ask him for contentment in being known and loved by God.

days.title: Public And Private
days.scripture: 1 SAMUEL 16:1-5 Now the LORD said to Samuel, “You have mourned long enough for Saul. I have rejected him as king of Israel, so fill your flask with olive oil and go to Bethlehem. Find a man named Jesse who lives there, for I have selected one of his sons to be my king.” But Samuel asked, “How can I do that? If Saul hears about it, he will kill me.” “Take a heifer with you,” the LORD replied, “and say that you have come to make a sacrifice to the LORD. Invite Jesse to the sacrifice, and I will show you which of his sons to anoint for me.” So Samuel did as the LORD instructed. When he arrived at Bethlehem, the elders of the town came trembling to meet him. “What’s wrong?” they asked. “Do you come in peace?” “Yes,” Samuel replied. “I have come to sacrifice to the LORD. Purify yourselves and come with me to the sacrifice.” Then Samuel performed the purification rite for Jesse and his sons and invited them to the sacrifice, too.
days.reflection: It may not feel like it, but these years are the most important years of your life. Believe it or not, the stuff you do as a teenager will set the direction of your life and determine your destiny. Most people think the teenage years are for having fun, exploring, and trying new things. And in some sense, that is true. But there is more happening than that. These are the years that you develop skills and character that serve you for the rest of your life. Do you remember this verse from Ecclesiastes 12? Remember your Creator in the days of your youth, before the days of trouble come and the years approach when you will say, “I find no pleasure in them”— King David had no idea that he was going to become the most famous man in Israel. But he didn’t let that stop him. David was isolated from his brothers and sent out into the fields to watch sheep. But he didn’t let that stop him either. He used the time he had as a teenager to set the habits and patterns of his life. He learned how to worship on his own, away from the Tabernacle. He learned to care for something other than himself - his father’s sheep. And he learned to be courageous, fighting off lions and bears. Think about how wild that is. Have you ever been out hiking and bumped into a bear? Imagine one coming for your sheep? Yet as a teenager, he defended the sheep and killed these massive animals. No one was there giving him a standing ovation for doing this. But God saw it. He was faithful with a little, and he was given a lot. Psalms 78:70- 72 says: He chose his servant David, calling him from the sheep pens. He took David from tending the ewes and lambs and made him the shepherd of Jacob’s descendants— God’s own people, Israel. He cared for them with a true heart and led them with skillful hands. God took David from the pen to the palace because David had a love for God and integrity in his teenage years. God saw how well David loved and cared for the sheep, and he knew he could be trusted with larger responsibility. What David did in private was rewarded in public. It will be the same for you. Have you ever heard the phrase,“Champions aren’t made on the field, they are revealed on the field”? Meaning, it's what’s done in private and during practice that makes someone perform well in public. Pay careful attention to who you are becoming right now. Pay attention to what you do when you don’t think anyone is looking because the truth is - God is working to train you in small things so he can trust you with larger things.
questions: Are there any little habits in your life that you think may get you in trouble later on if you don’t sort them out? If so, what are they, and what sort of damage do you think they could do later on?
Read this quote: "Sow a thought, reap an action; sow an action, reap a habit; sow a habit, reap a character; sow a character, reap a destiny." How does this specifically apply to the teenage years?
Ask the person you are doing this study with about one thing they learned in their teenage years that has helped them and one thing that has hurt them.

thoughts.verse: “If you are faithful in little things, you will be faithful in large ones. But if you are dishonest in little things, you won’t be honest with greater responsibilities. Luke 16:10 Read through his verse slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to give you a careful heart to cultivate godly habits at this stage of your life. Ask him for power and grace in any areas that you are struggling in.

days.title: What God Wants
days.scripture: 1 SAMUEL 16:4-13 So Samuel did as the LORD instructed. When he arrived at Bethlehem, the elders of the town came trembling to meet him. “What’s wrong?” they asked. “Do you come in peace?” “Yes,” Samuel replied. “I have come to sacrifice to the LORD. Purify yourselves and come with me to the sacrifice.” Then Samuel performed the purification rite for Jesse and his sons and invited them to the sacrifice, too. When they arrived, Samuel took one look at Eliab and thought, “Surely this is the LORD’s anointed!” But the LORD said to Samuel, “Don’t judge by his appearance or height, for I have rejected him. The LORD doesn’t see things the way you see them. People judge by outward appearance, but the LORD looks at the heart.” Then Jesse told his son Abinadab to step forward and walk in front of Samuel. But Samuel said, “This is not the one the LORD has chosen.” Next Jesse summoned Shimea, but Samuel said, “Neither is this the one the LORD has chosen.” In the same way all seven of Jesse’s sons were presented to Samuel. But Samuel said to Jesse, “The LORD has not chosen any of these.” Then Samuel asked, “Are these all the sons you have?” “There is still the youngest,” Jesse replied. “But he’s out in the fields watching the sheep and goats.” “Send for him at once,” Samuel said. “We will not sit down to eat until he arrives.” So Jesse sent for him. He was dark and handsome, with beautiful eyes. And the LORD said, “This is the one; anoint him.” So as David stood there among his brothers, Samuel took the flask of olive oil he had brought and anointed David with the oil. And the Spirit of the LORD came powerfully upon David from that day on. Then Samuel returned to Ramah.
days.reflection: What are the most impressive characteristics people are attracted to in the world today? Money? Power? A good body? Popularity? Humor? It's kind of crazy what makes people pay attention today. Our society is always impressed with what can be seen. This can put a lot of pressure on guys your age to feel like they have to live up to some kind of impossible standard. You have to suck it up. You have to be tough. You have to know how to make girls like you. You have to be good at sports. You have to be super smart. You have to get into the right college. Pressure, pressure, pressure. Even goldy people can fall into this trap. Samuel kept looking at the outward appearance of David’s brothers. He was attracted to the wrong things. Being tall, jacked, or charismatic may impress people, but they are not factors God needs to use someone. Becoming a real man is more about your inner life than your looks. It's more about your heart than your appearance. God warned Samuel not to fall into the trap of judging people by the world’s standards. Don’t judge by his appearance or height, for I have rejected him. The LORD doesn’t see things the way you see them. People judge by outward appearance, but the LORD looks at the heart.” Gods cares about your heart. Love: God looks for men who have love in their hearts - love for Him, love for others, and love for themselves. Humility: God wants men who are humble and recognize that they need His guidance and help in their lives. Faith: God looks for men who have faith in Him and trust Him even when things are difficult or uncertain. Obedience: God wants men who are obedient to His commands and follow His ways. Compassion: God looks for men who show compassion and kindness to others, especially those who are hurting or in need. Forgiveness: God wants men who are willing to forgive others and seek forgiveness themselves. Integrity: God looks for men who have integrity and live their lives with honesty, fairness, and moral courage. It’s not wrong to look your best, get good grades, or get in good shape. It's just that these aren’t the most important thing. Don’t neglect growing your relationship with God for the sake of getting other people to like you. When you look back on your teenage years, you will be remembered for your integrity and kindness more than anything else. Make a commitment now to grow your heart as you grow in the other areas of your life. That is definitely what matters most.
questions: What is your definition of integrity? (You can think about it for a bit before you answer)
Do you think that guys your age focus more on their hearts or their appearance? Why?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share what they think God looks for in a man. How have they gone about developing that in their own life?

thoughts.verse: So we have stopped evaluating others from a human point of view. At one time we thought of Christ merely from a human point of view. How differently we know him now! This means that anyone who belongs to Christ has become a new person. The old life is gone; a new life has begun! 2 Corinthians 5:16-17 Read through his verse slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to give you a heart after him. A heart that wants to please God, not just have the attention of others.

days.title: Attention And Anointing
days.scripture: 1 SAMUEL 16:11-13 Then Samuel asked, “Are these all the sons you have?” “There is still the youngest,” Jesse replied. “But he’s out in the fields watching the sheep and goats.” “Send for him at once,” Samuel said. “We will not sit down to eat until he arrives.” So Jesse sent for him. He was dark and handsome, with beautiful eyes. And the LORD said, “This is the one; anoint him.” So as David stood there among his brothers, Samuel took the flask of olive oil he had brought and anointed David with the oil. And the Spirit of the LORD came powerfully upon David from that day on. Then Samuel returned to Ramah.
days.reflection: If you view life as a giant competition, you will constantly feel like you have to do whatever it takes to win. The older you get and the more you embrace that mentality, the more you will be tempted to cheat. And there is nothing worse than being exposed in public for cheating. When athletes take steroids, politicians pad their resumes, or husbands cheat on their wives, it always leads to guilt and shame. It’s never worth cheating to get ahead. What you need in life to succeed is God's favor. And he gives favor to men whose hearts he can trust. Did you notice what happened when David was anointed by Samuel? Samuel took the flask of olive oil he had brought and anointed David with the oil. And the Spirit of the LORD came powerfully upon David from that day on David’s life is about to change forever after he is anointed. He is going to be catapulted from obscurity to public attention. But David has proved in secret that he is ready to be used by God in public. When it comes to getting influence in life, you have three real options. Number one, you can hustle. You can scramble and strive and cheat and scheme and work and slave to try and get ahead. You can rely on yourself and your own abilities and your own efforts and hope that it is enough. But this ultimately leads to exhaustion and frustration. You will be tempted to compromise and cheat to keep up. The second option is you can rely on luck. You can go through life passively hoping that things will work out. You can have a carefree attitude and not worry about anything. But this isn't how life works either. Passivity doesn't lead to godliness or favor or fruit in your life. The third option is to ask God for his favor. When the Spirit of God comes on David, a new kind of power flows into his life. Now I want to make a comment here that is really important for you to understand: at this point in David's life, he is a teenager pretty close to your age. He isn't some massive grown man in his 30s or 40s. He is really young. He is the youngest brother, the runt of his pack. Yet with God's help, he is able to leapfrog the natural order to the forefront of God's purposes in his life. Don’t rely on your ability to hustle. Don’t rely on luck. Ask God to anoint you with his Spirit and ask for his help. Surrender your need to make it on your own and you will be amazed at what God can do through you.
questions: Have you ever cheated on something and gotten caught? If you have, what was it and how did you feel?
Have you ever felt God empower you or help you in any way?
Ask the person you are doing this study with about a season or moment where they felt the Holy Spirit helping them. What was it, and how did they know it was God?

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. “You fathers—if your children ask for a fish, do you give them a snake instead? Or if they ask for an egg, do you give them a scorpion? Of course not! So if you sinful people know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him.”
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in any of them?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to invite the Holy Spirit to fill you with fresh power. Ask him for help in any areas you may feel stuck.

days.scripture: 1 SAMUEL 16:11-13 Then Samuel asked, “Are these all the sons you have?” “There is still the youngest,” Jesse replied. “But he’s out in the fields watching the sheep and goats.” “Send for him at once,” Samuel said. “We will not sit down to eat until he arrives.” So Jesse sent for him. He was dark and handsome, with beautiful eyes. And the LORD said, “This is the one; anoint him.” So as David stood there among his brothers, Samuel took the flask of olive oil he had brought and anointed David with the oil. And the Spirit of the LORD came powerfully upon David from that day on. Then Samuel returned to Ramah.
days.reflection: This week, you have learned a really important lesson about becoming a man. You have learned to contrast the world's way of striving for attention versus God's way of getting attention. Striving or surrendering; this is the choice you will have to make as you go forward. If you choose to strive for attention, you will live a life of pressure and performance. You will have to constantly come up with ways to get people’s attention, constantly do things to impress others, constantly feel the pressure to do well, or get left out or left behind. But if you choose to please God and worry about what he thinks of you, you will be free from the anxiety of having to keep up with everyone else. When God can trust you to love him, care about others, and not become prideful or selfish, then you become the kind of person he can really use. And when you are ready, he can call you in a moment. Think about these men who were called out of obscurity into shaping history. Moses was called to set a nation free. Joseph was called to save a nation from famine. Daniel was called to be faithful in exile. Paul was called from persecuting the church to being an apostle. Peter was called from fishing to preaching. Don’t rush for attention. Jesus spent 30 years building his relationship with his Father before he moved into his 3 years of public ministry. 90 percent of Jesus’ life was in faithful, everyday work. Then when he was ready - bam - he was anointed with power and the world has never been the same. Psalms 75:6-7 reminds us of this: “No one from the east or the west or from the desert can exalt themselves. It is God who judges: He brings one down, he exalts another.” God has a call on your life. Surrender yourself to him, and he will take care of the rest. He can take you from being a normal teenager in an everyday life and bring you into a life of adventure and faith. Worry about integrity and being faithful in the small things. Know that your father sees what you do in the secret place, and he will bring you to the public place when the time is right. Man looks at the outward appearance, but God looks at the heart. Focus on that, and he will take care of the rest.
questions: If you could have lunch with any famous person in the world, who would it be and why?
What sort of questions would you like to ask him or her?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share a couple of ways they have learned to trust God and surrender their need for recognition.

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. In the same way, you who are younger must accept the authority of the elders. And all of you, dress yourselves in humility as you relate to one another, for “God opposes the proud but gives grace to the humble.” So humble yourselves under the mighty power of God, and at the right time he will lift you up in honor.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in any of them?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to freshly surrender yourself to God. Share any anxieties you may feel about being left out or overlooked to him and ask for his comfort and help.

extras: WEEK 2 MANSCHOOL PAYING ATTENTION MAN SCHOOL REMINDER The recipe for fun is pretty simple when you’re raising boys: add to any activity an element of danger, stir in a little exploration, add a dash of destruction, and you’ve got yourself a winner. - John Eldredge OPTION 1. ACTIVITY: PAYING ATTENTION Our desire for attention has made us slaves to our phones. People can’t even go 5 minutes without checking in on social media. The typical person touches their phone over 3000 times a day. Go to a food court at a local mall and enjoy a great lunch together. Then look around and point out how addicted people are to their phones. The majority of people are watching social media. Point out how crazy this actually is, and how you don’t need to get caught up in that cycle. Make sure you put your own phone away ☺ OPTION 2. WATCH ORIGINAL SPIDERMAN With great power comes great responsibility. An ordinary kid learns character and humility as he is thrust into a place of power and prominence. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=t06RUxPbp_c OPTION 3. DINNER AND DISCUSSION Take your son out to one of his favorite restaurants or for desserts and talk through what it means to entrust your story into God's hands. How can we learn to live for an audience of one? Have him do some research on the “Hawthorne Effect,” which is the idea that we change how we act when we think other people are watching. Ask him in what ways he changes how he acts based on who is around.
FIELD NOTES PRAYER FOR MY SON
